rationale: Ansari, Essegaier, and Kohli (2000) is treated as a central prior attribute-based recommender model in the literature review and is reused in the citing paper's model setup (e.g., consumer and product intercept structure). The citing paper also operationalizes an Ansari-style specification as a benchmark (Model 6) and evaluates its own gains relative to it. So the citation is doing real conceptual and methodological work in framing and positioning the contribution, not just a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the Ansari et al. recommender framework by adding a virtual-expert latent residual component to capture unobserved product attributes. It then benchmarks this extension against the Ansari-style model to show improved predictive performance.
